summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is total internal reflection (TIR)?

A reflection flips a figure over a line (axis of reflection). The image is the same distance from the line as the original, on the opposite side. The answer is All light reflected when hitting boundary above the critical angle. The other options — 'Light passing through all materials', 'Light absorbed at a boundary', and 'Partial reflection only' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the formula for the critical angle?

Critical Angle sin θ_c = n₂/n₁ (where n₁ > n₂). Total Internal Reflection (TIR) Occurs when light travels from a denser to a less dense medium at an angle greater than the critical angle. The other options — 'sin θ_c = n₁/n₂', 'cos θ_c = n₂/n₁', and 'tan θ_c = n₂/n₁' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
